subject: Cloud Computing
title: What 'Cloud' Actually Means
description: A quick win: understand What 'Cloud' Actually Means.

hook.text: The word 'cloud' came from network diagrams where engineers drew a cloud to mean 'not our problem.'

buildup.text: Cloud computing means using someone else's computers over the internet, on demand.

discovery.text: The key innovation: you pay only for what you use, and you can scale instantly.

twist.text: There's no single 'cloud.' It's millions of servers in data centers owned by a few giants.

climax.text: The cloud is not magic. It's real hardware in real buildings, managed by someone else.

punchline.text: The cloud is just someone else's computer. Really.

quiz.question: What does 'cloud computing' fundamentally mean?
options: Using remote computers over the internet on a pay-as-you-go basis
Storing data in the atmosphere using radio waves
A type of computer that floats between different locations
